design_philosophy.core_loop: Hop diagonally across isometric pyramid cubes to change them to target color while avoiding enemies; progress when all cubes match target state
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Spatial navigation pressure from pursuing enemies (especially Coily) combined with puzzle logic of managing cube states (single/double hits, reverting mechanics)
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Nimble platformer protagonist navigating impossible geometry while evading threats through quick directional changes
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Strict isometric perspective forces diagonal-only movement; no continuous running—pure hopping creates rhythm-based play; enemies actively undo progress (Slick/Sam) rather than just threatening collision, adding puzzle layer to avoidance

difficulty.lives_system: Multiple lives with collision-based loss (contact with Coily, Ugg, Wrongway, or red balls = 1 life lost)
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based progression; completing all cube states advances to next stage with increased complexity
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Limited; difficulty increases automatically across stages through cube state variations (single-hit to double-hit to cycling colors) and enemy behavior escalation. Escape discs provide tactical relief valve when Coily pursues.

progression.unlock_mechanism: Stage unlock via cube completion; later stages introduce mechanical modifiers (cube double-hits, color cycling) rather than new areas
replayability_hooks: Enemy behavior patterns reward pattern recognition
Bonus points for unused discs incentivize risk management

world_building_techniques: Character personality through sound design (synthesized 'swearing' via grawlix speech balloons on collision)
Enemy archetypes establish implicit threat hierarchy (named enemies with distinct behaviors)

boss_design.philosophy: Primary antagonist Coily (purple snake) represents escalating threat rather than discrete boss encounter; transforms from egg to pursuer and can be exploited via disc mechanic for points. No traditional boss fights; progression through stage completion.
notable_bosses: Coily - Arch-nemesis; pursues Q*bert and can be defeated by disc trap (500 points)

faction_indicators: Purple enemies (Coily, Ugg, Wrongway) = lethal threats
danger_signals: Coily egg spawning indicates imminent snake pursuit threat
Green ball arrival = cube reversion danger
environmental_storytelling: Isometric pyramid = impossible space suggesting otherworldly setting
Color-change mechanic positions Q*bert as reality-altering agent
Escape discs as environmental safe zones reinforcing verticality as survival resource

sources_used: wikipedia - Q*bert arcade/general overview, gameplay mechanics, enemy behaviors, progression structure
tcrf - NES port information, developer (Konami/Ultra Games), 1989 release date
